subject: Public Health
title: Disease X: Preparing for the Unknown
description: A micro-lesson that makes Disease X: Preparing for the Unknown usable.

hook.text: The WHO's priority disease list includes 'Disease X'—a placeholder for a pathogen that doesn't exist yet.

buildup.text: Disease X represents an unknown virus that could emerge from animals, labs, or environmental change.

discovery.text: Platform technologies like mRNA can be rapidly adapted to new pathogens—a key preparedness tool.

twist.text: COVID-19 was a Disease X event. The next one could be more lethal, more transmissible, or both.

climax.text: Preparing for the unknown requires flexible systems, not rigid plans for specific diseases.

punchline.text: The next pandemic won't wait for us to be ready.

quiz.question: What is Disease X on the WHO's priority list?
options: A placeholder for an unknown future pathogen
